faq.question: What is a flash guide number?
faq.answer: The guide number (GN) indicates flash power. It equals flash-to-subject distance × f-number at ISO 100. A GN of 36 means the flash can illuminate a subject 36 meters away at f/1 (ISO 100).

faq.question: How does ISO affect flash range?
faq.answer: Doubling ISO increases effective flash range by √2 (about 1.4×). Going from ISO 100 to 400 doubles your flash distance.

faq.question: What is a good guide number for a flash?
faq.answer: Built-in flashes: GN 8-12. Mid-range speedlights: GN 30-40. Professional speedlights: GN 50-60. Studio strobes can exceed GN 100.

faq.question: Is the Flash Guide Number Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard photography formulas trusted by photographers. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Flash Guide Number Calculator?
sections.body: The Flash Guide Number Calculator is a free online tool designed for photographers who need quick, accurate calculations in the photography space. By entering your guide number, aperture, iso, you get instant results including max flash distance, required f-stop at 5m, effective gn at iso. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting max flash distance right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In photography, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by photographers worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to capture better images with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your guide number and need to find the right max flash distance. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

tips: Double-check your guide number before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
